📋 Key Notes
FieldAssist · Feature Documentation

Incentive Module

The Incentive Module enables organizations to configure, manage, and deliver performance-based incentives to their sales teams. Admins design rules from the Dashboard — Sales Users track progress in real time through the GT App, with full visibility into earnings, qualifiers, criteria, and slabs.

⏱️ ~5 min to complete setup
👥 Admin + Sales User flows
📱 Dashboard + GT App
How to Access the Incentive Module
Complete these steps before creating your first incentive rule
1
Enable via Settings
CST Admin enables the Incentive Module from Company Settings
2
Open Dashboard Sidebar
Click Incentive in the left nav to expand it into two sub-items
3
KPI Subscription First
Subscribe the required KPIs before creating any rule
4
Incentive Rule
Build, configure, and manage incentive rules for your cohorts
Dashboard Navigation
Incentive → KPI Subscription + Incentive Rule
Step 1 of 2 · Prerequisites
KPI Subscription
Before building any incentive rule, Admins must subscribe the required KPIs from the KPI Subscription page. This maps a Global Outlet Metric (GOM) to a named KPI that can then be selected during rule creation.
📊
Global Outlet Metric (GOM)
The underlying data source driven by SQL logic. Admins select the GOM and give it an internal name.
🎯
Qualifier vs. Standard KPI
A KPI can be a Qualifier (performance gate) or a Standard metric for reward calculation — controlled via the Is Qualifier toggle.
🔗
Target Subscription
Optionally link the KPI to a Flexible Target for achievement-vs-target tracking in the GT App.
📋
KPI Subscription — List Page
Shows all subscribed KPIs. Use the Inactive toggle to view deactivated entries. Click + Create to subscribe a new KPI.
Dashboard Screenshot
The KPI Subscription list. Each row is one subscribed metric available for use in the Incentive Rule creation flow.
ColumnWhat it means
NameInternal KPI name visible in Incentive Rule configuration dropdowns.
GOM NameThe Global Outlet Metric this KPI subscribes to — the actual underlying data source.
IsQualifierKPITrue = qualifier gate. False = used for reward calculation.
OperatorFor Qualifier KPIs — the comparison condition (≥, ≤, =, >, <) applied against the Target value.
TargetThe threshold value compared against KPI achievement when used as a qualifier.
Achievement TypeHow the KPI value is expressed: Currency, Unit, StandardUnit, SuperUnit, Integer, Decimal, Qty, or Percentage.
Target NameIf linked to a Flexible Target, the target name shows here.
ActionsAllows the Admin to Deactivate a subscription.
➕
Creating a New KPI Subscription
Click + Create on the list page. Slide through the screenshots to see the full creation form including GOM metric details.
Dashboard Screenshots
The creation form — Name, KPI Dropdown (GOM), Achievement Type, Target Subscription, and Is Qualifier toggle.
Once a GOM is selected, the Global Outlet Metric Details panel expands — showing Metric Name, Description, Data Type, Perspective, Query Relation, and the Report SQL Query for your review.
1 / 2
FieldRequiredDescription
Name RequiredDisplay name of this KPI as it will appear in Incentive Rule creation dropdowns.
KPI Dropdown (GOM) RequiredSelect the Global Outlet Metric to subscribe. Once selected, the GOM details panel expands showing the metric's description, data type, and SQL query for review.
Achievement Type RequiredHow the KPI value is expressed: Currency, Unit, StandardUnit, SuperUnit, Integer, Decimal, Qty, or Percentage.
Target SubscriptionOptionalLinks this KPI to a Flexible Target, enabling target vs. achievement tracking in the GT App.
Is QualifierToggleWhen enabled, this KPI becomes a Qualifier KPI. Two additional fields appear: Comparison Operator and Target Value.
🔍
SQL Query Block: When you select a GOM, the related SQL Query is displayed. Inspect it to understand exactly what data this metric pulls before subscribing — enabling fully informed decisions.
Step 2 of 2 · Rule Creation Wizard
Creating an Incentive Rule
After subscribing KPIs, click Incentive Rule in the sidebar and then + Create to launch the 7-step wizard. Use the tabs to jump freely between steps.
1Basic Info
2Rule Config
3Qualifier
4Criteria
5Slabs
6List
7Review
📝
Step 1 – Basic Information
Set the identity of this rule. The Rule Name is internal; the Display Name is what Sales Users see in the GT App.
Dashboard Screenshot — Rule Details
Step 1 of Basic Information. Fill Rule Name, Display Name, and optional Description. Click Rule Configuration to proceed.
FieldRequiredDescription
What would you like to name this rule?RequiredInternal reference name — visible to Admins only.
How should this rule appear to others?RequiredThe display name visible to Sales Users on their program cards in the GT App.
Can you briefly describe what this rule does?OptionalA short explanation of the rule's purpose for Admins and Users.
Attach Additional DetailsOptionalAttach a supporting file (JPG, JPEG, PNG, PDF · max 10 MB).
➡️
Click Rule Configuration to proceed to Step 2 — frequency, cohort, dates, and the leaderboard toggle.
Step 1 of 7
⚙️
Step 2 – Rule Configuration
Define when this rule runs, who it applies to, and how long it stays active.
Dashboard Screenshot — Rule Configuration
Frequency, Cohort selection, Show Leaderboard toggle, and the active date range.
FieldRequiredDescription
How often should this rule be applied?RequiredEvaluation frequency: Monthly (calendar month) or Custom (date range).
Which team or cohort should this rule apply to?RequiredSelect a pre-configured cohort of Sales Users.
Show Leaderboard?ToggleWhen ON, a real-time rank view based on reward achievement becomes visible in the GT App for this rule.
When should this rule start?RequiredThe date from which this incentive rule becomes active.
When should this rule end?RequiredThe date on which this incentive rule expires.
🔵 Add Rule Qualifier: ON
  • Next → Rule Qualifier step
  • Set qualifier KPIs that gate the entire rule
⚪ Add Rule Qualifier: OFF
  • Next → Criteria Configuration directly
  • No qualifying gate — all cohort users are eligible
Step 2 of 7
🔒
Step 3 – Rule Qualifier
A qualifier gates the entire rule — users must meet the condition to be eligible for any reward. Only appears if the Qualifier toggle was enabled in Step 2.
Dashboard Screenshot — Rule Qualifier
The Rule Qualifier page. The KPI dropdown shows only KPIs subscribed with IsQualifierKPI = True, with their pre-defined operator and target.
🔵 Multiple Qualifiers: ON
  • Select a Condition — AND or OR
  • Add qualifier KPIs via + Add Another Qualifier
  • AND = user must meet ALL qualifiers
  • OR = meeting ANY one qualifier is sufficient
⚪ Multiple Qualifiers: OFF
  • Select exactly one qualifier KPI
  • Operator and target inherited from KPI Subscription
⚠️
Only Qualifier KPIs appear here. If a KPI is missing, go to KPI Subscription and create it with Is Qualifier enabled.
Step 3 of 7
🎯
Step 4 – Criteria Configuration
Criteria are the performance dimensions within a rule. Each has its own KPI, optional qualifier, and slab configuration. A rule can have multiple criteria.
Dashboard Screenshot — Criteria Setup
Criteria Setup — name the criterion, select the criteria KPI (non-qualifier KPIs only), and optionally enable a criteria-level qualifier.
FieldRequiredDescription
What would you like to name this criteria?RequiredInternal reference name, e.g. "Monthly Sales Volume" or "New Outlet Addition".
What criteria KPI would you like to use?RequiredSelect from subscribed KPIs where IsQualifierKPI = False. These are standard reward-calculation metrics.
Would you like to add criteria qualifier?ToggleEnable to add a qualifier for this criterion independently — follows the same AND/OR logic as the Rule Qualifier step.
➡️
Click Slab Configuration to define the reward calculation for this criterion.
Step 4 of 7
📈
Step 5 – Slab Configuration
Define how rewards are calculated for this criteria. Slabs break performance into tiers — each with a threshold and a corresponding reward.
Dashboard Screenshot — Slab Configuration
Slab Configuration — select the calculation method, optionally enable a reward cap, then define each slab with its Constraint Value, Reward Quantity, and Reward Type.
How should the reward be calculated?
Select a calculation method — click any button below to open a detailed explanation with formula and example.
Slab Fields — What to fill in for each slab:
FieldRequiredDescription
Constraint Value RequiredThe performance threshold for this slab. The user must reach this value to qualify for the reward (e.g. ₹50,000 of invoice value, or 100 cases).
Reward Quantity RequiredThe reward amount for this slab. Interpretation depends on the calculation method — flat points, a multiplier value, or a percentage.
Reward Type RequiredHow the reward is expressed: Points, Currency, or other configured reward types.
+ Add SlabActionAdds another performance tier. Each slab creates an additional band with its own constraint and reward.
Reward CapOptionalMaximum earnings ceiling. Even if the user exceeds the highest slab, they will not earn beyond this cap.
💡
Highest slab only: When a user crosses multiple slab thresholds, only the highest slab achieved is rewarded — not a cumulative sum.
Step 5 of 7
📑
Step 6 – Criteria List
After configuring each criterion it appears here. Add more criteria, edit or delete existing ones before submitting.
Dashboard Screenshot — Criteria List
The Criteria List showing a configured criterion with Name, KPI, Qualifier status, Reward Cap, and Slab details. Use the pencil to edit or trash to delete.
✅
Add multiple criteria to give users more ways to earn. Each criterion is tracked independently. Click Add Another Criteria to restart the Criteria Configuration flow for a new dimension.
Step 6 of 7
✅
Step 7 – Review & Submit
A full consolidated summary of everything configured. Review thoroughly before submitting — once submitted the rule is immediately active.
Dashboard Screenshot — Review Page
The Review page shows Basic Information (rule name, display name, description, frequency, cohort, date range, qualifier status), Rule Qualifier details, and Criteria summary. Click any section heading to jump back and edit.
⚠️
Review carefully before submitting. Verify rule name, display name, cohort, date range, qualifiers, all criteria, and slab configurations.
🖊️
Jump to Edit
Click any section header on the Review page to jump directly to that step for edits.
🚀
Submit
Rule created and active for the specified cohort within the configured date range.
Step 7 of 7
Sales User Experience
GT App – Accessing Incentives
After completing Day Start, Sales Users navigate to My Incentive via the hamburger menu. The home screen shows an Incentive Summary widget for a quick overview. Click any phone frame to see a full detail view.
🏠
Home Screen – Incentive Summary Widget
Shows current earnings and rank at a glance. View varies based on whether Admin enabled Leaderboard. Scroll inside the phone frame to see the full screen.
Tap to expand ⤢
Leaderboard Enabled
Rank CTA visible
Tap to expand ⤢
Leaderboard Disabled
Rank CTA hidden
1
Incentive Summary Card
Shows earnings from all active rules + potential ceiling. Tap to open the My Incentives page.
2
Rank CTA
Visible only when Admin enabled the Leaderboard toggle for a rule. Tap to open the Leaderboard for that specific rule.
My Incentives
Earnings Dashboard
The My Incentives page gives a complete view of active and past incentive programs. Access via the home summary card or the left navigation menu.
💰
Top Section — Earnings Summary
Two summary cards provide a macro-level view of all earnings. Scroll inside the phone frame to see the full screen.
Tap to expand ⤢
My Incentives
Full view
Tap to expand ⤢
Criteria cards
expanded
Total Incentive
₹52K
Earned from closed programs in the current Financial Year.
→
Ongoing Incentive
₹27K
From currently active programs with potential ceiling shown.
🗂️
Rule Cards — My Programs
Each active incentive rule appears as a card. Cards expand to show criteria-level detail.
📊
Qualification Status
Each card shows Qualified or Not Qualified. Tap the qualifier tag to open a bottom sheet.
₹
Reward Breakdown
Pending Reward: Earned within a criteria but not yet qualified.
Total Reward: All rewards earned so far (qualified + non-qualified).
Potential: Maximum possible earnings from this rule.

When a criteria is qualified, Pending drops to ₹0 and moves into Total Reward.
🏆
Rank / Leaderboard
If the Admin enabled the Leaderboard toggle for this rule, the user's current rank appears on the rule card. Tapping the rank badge opens the full Leaderboard screen for that rule.
Interactive Drill-Downs
Modals, Bottom Sheets & Detail Views
Tapping various elements opens contextual detail views. Click any phone below to explore what each modal shows — images are scrollable inside the phone frame.

tap qualifier tag
Tap to expand ⤢

tap View Slabs

tap i icon

Leaderboard Screen
🏆
How to reach this screen: On the Home screen or inside a Rule Card in My Incentives, tap the Rank badge (only visible when Admin has enabled the Leaderboard toggle for that rule). This opens the full Leaderboard page for that specific rule.
Tap to expand ⤢
Leaderboard
Rank view
🥇
Podium View
Top 3 performers in a podium layout with names and total reward values.
#
Your Rank & Gap
User's rank and value shown prominently. A motivational message shows how much more is needed to reach the next rank.
👥
All Participants
Scrollable list in rank order. Logged-in user highlighted with a "You" tag in blue.
Reference
Key Notes & Considerations
⚙️
Module Enablement: CST must enable the Incentive Module at company level in Settings before any configuration is possible.
👥
Cohort Pre-requisite: All cohorts must be defined and active before creating incentive rules. Users not in a configured cohort will not see any programs in the GT App.
📅
Data Lag: Sales Users see incentive data with an N-1 day lag. Today's achievements reflect in the GT App the following day.
📋
Review Before Submit: Admin should review the final summary thoroughly before submitting. Rules cannot be partially activated once submitted.
AH
